summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Process Step mapping feature for Equipment Manager. All backend API tests passed (7/7). All frontend UI tests passed - Process Step field is correctly visible ONLY at subunit and maintainable_item levels, NOT visible at installation/plant_unit/section_system/equipment_unit levels. Process Step inheritance from parent works correctly. Process Step can be edited (free text input).
critical_code_review_comments: Backend equipment.py correctly implements process_step inheritance at lines 241-249 - inherits from parent only for subunit/maintainable_item levels
Backend iso14224_models.py correctly includes process_step field in EquipmentNodeCreate and EquipmentNodeUpdate models
Frontend PropertiesPanel.js correctly shows Process Step field only for subunit and maintainable_item levels (lines 151-165)
Frontend shows 'Inherited by child items' hint when process_step has a value
Translations properly defined in LanguageContext.js for both EN (lines 466-470) and NL (lines 1511-1515)
seed_data_creation: Main agent created test hierarchy: ProcessStep Test Plant > Extrusion Unit > Polymer Processing > Extruder E-101 > Barrel Assembly (process_step='Extrusion') > Screw Bearing (inherited process_step='Extrusion')
context_for_next_testing_agent: Process Step mapping feature fully tested. The field is visible ONLY at subunit and maintainable_item levels in the Properties Panel. Children inherit process_step from parent when created. Process Step can be edited via free text input and changes are saved via PATCH API.

backend_api_tests.Process step inheritance from parent: PASSED - child inherits process_step when not provided
backend_api_tests.Process step override after inheritance: PASSED - inherited value can be changed
backend_api_tests.Clear process_step: PASSED - can set to empty string
